identity.name: John Doe
identity.background: Experienced full-stack developer with a healthcare informatics focus. Strong preference for clean architecture and test-driven development. Uses AI regularly for prototyping, code review, and technical research.
identity.profession: Senior Software Engineer / Backend Systems (healthcare platforms)

ongoing_projects.name: Unique Profile (portable AI user profile via MCP)
ongoing_projects.notes: Building a user-owned profile/memory layer that can be injected into any LLM session. Focus: portability, safety (confirmed memories), and practical integration.

ongoing_projects.name: Patient data pipeline modernization
ongoing_projects.notes: Migrating legacy ETL jobs to event-driven architecture; emphasis on HIPAA compliance and data lineage tracking.

ongoing_projects.name: Home workshop CNC setup
ongoing_projects.notes: Configuring a benchtop CNC router for precision woodworking, dialing in feed rates and toolpath optimization.

ongoing_projects.name: Python tooling & deployment workflows
ongoing_projects.notes: Regular work with Python APIs/services, containers, and CI/CD patterns; prefers minimal, maintainable solutions over heavy abstractions.

memories.content: Prefers answers that are direct and practical; dislikes over-engineered solutions unless explicitly requested.
Codes in Python daily and typically wants minimal working code first, then optional improvements.
Often uses AI as a tutor: wants step-by-step reasoning for learning topics, and prefers not to receive final answers to homework-style problems.
Strong interest in portable personalization across LLMs: wants a user-owned identity/preferences layer that can be reused across vendors and models.
Values precision and correctness over 'nice' phrasing; prefers explicit assumptions when the problem is ambiguous.
Maintains a home workshop with CNC equipment and frequently optimizes toolpaths and material settings.
Background includes healthcare informatics; often discusses data pipelines, compliance requirements, and system integration patterns.
